ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By those [angels] who extract with violence
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By those ˹angels˺ stripping out ˹evil souls˺ harshly,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] those who remove with ease
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those pulling out ˹good souls˺ gently,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] those who glide [as if] swimming
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those gliding ˹through heavens˺ swiftly,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who race each other in a race
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those taking the lead vigorously,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who arrange [each] matter,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those conducting affairs ˹obediently˺!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On the Day the blast [of the Horn] will convulse [creation],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Consider˺ the Day ˹when˺ the quaking Blast will come to pass,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There will follow it the subsequent [one].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: followed by a second Blast.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Hearts, that Day, will tremble,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The deniers’˺ hearts on that Day will be trembling ˹in horror˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Their eyes humbled.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: with their eyes downcast.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They are [presently] saying, "Will we indeed be returned to [our] former state [of life]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹But now˺ they ask ˹mockingly˺, “Will we really be restored to our former state,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Even if we should be decayed bones?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: even after we have been reduced to decayed bones?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They say, "That, then, would be a losing return."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Adding, “Then such a return would be a ˹total˺ loss ˹for us˺!”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it will be but one shout,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But indeed, it will take only one ˹mighty˺ Blast,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And suddenly they will be [alert] upon the earth's surface.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and at once they will be above ground.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has there reached you the story of Moses? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has the story of Moses reached you ˹O Prophet˺?

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ نَادَىٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ بِٱلۡوَادِ ٱلۡمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith nadahu rabbuhu bialwadi almuqaddasi tuwan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When his Lord called to him in the sacred valley of Tuwa,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: His Lord called him in the sacred valley of Ṭuwa,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹commanding,˺ “Go to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ˹all bounds˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَقُلۡ هَل لَّكَ إِلَىٰٓ أَن تَزَكَّىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqul hal laka ila an tazakka
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And say to him, 'Would you [be willing to] purify yourself
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And say, ‘Would you ˹be willing to˺ purify yourself,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And let me guide you to your Lord so you would fear [Him]?'"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and let me guide you to your Lord so that you will be in awe ˹of Him˺?’”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he showed him the greatest sign,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then Moses showed him the great sign,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But Pharaoh denied and disobeyed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but he denied and disobeyed ˹Allah˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he turned his back, striving.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then turned his back, striving ˹against the truth˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he gathered [his people] and called out
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he summoned ˹his people˺ and called out,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And said, "I am your most exalted lord."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: saying, “I am your lord, the most high!”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So Allah seized him in exemplary punishment for the last and the first [transgression].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So Allah overtook him, making him an example in this life and the next.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبۡرَةٗ لِّمَن يَخۡشَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laAAibratan liman yakhsha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that is a warning for whoever would fear [Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this is a lesson for whoever stands in awe of ˹Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: ءَأَنتُمۡ أَشَدُّ خَلۡقًا أَمِ ٱلسَّمَآءُۚ بَنَىٰهَا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aantum ashaddu khalqan ami alssamao banaha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Are you a more difficult creation or is the heaven? Allah constructed it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Which is harder to create: you or the sky? He built it,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He raised its ceiling and proportioned it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: raising it high and forming it flawlessly.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And He darkened its night and extracted its brightness.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He dimmed its night, and brought forth its daylight.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And after that He spread the earth.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for the earth, He spread it out as well,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He extracted from it its water and its pasture,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: bringing forth its water and pastures

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the mountains He set firmly
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and setting the mountains firmly ˹upon it˺—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As provision for you and your grazing livestock.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: all as ˹a means of˺ sustenance for you and your animals.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when there comes the greatest Overwhelming Calamity -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But, when the Supreme Disaster comes to pass—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day when man will remember that for which he strove,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Day every person will remember all ˹their˺ striving,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Hellfire will be exposed for [all] those who see -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the Hellfire will be displayed for all to see—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So as for he who transgressed
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then as for those who transgressed

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And preferred the life of the world,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and preferred the ˹fleeting˺ life of this world,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed, Hellfire will be [his] refuge.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Hellfire will certainly be ˹their˺ home.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّا مَنۡ خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِۦ وَنَهَى ٱلنَّفۡسَ عَنِ ٱلۡهَوَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waama man khafa maqama rabbihi wanaha alnnafsa AAani alhawa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But as for he who feared the position of his Lord and prevented the soul from [unlawful] inclination,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And as for those who were in awe of standing before their Lord and restrained themselves from ˹evil˺ desires,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed, Paradise will be [his] refuge.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Paradise will certainly be ˹their˺ home.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They ask you, [O Muhammad], about the Hour: when is its arrival?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They ask you ˹O Prophet˺ regarding the Hour, “When will it be?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In what [position] are you that you should mention it?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But it is not for you to tell its time.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To your Lord is its finality.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That knowledge rests with your Lord ˹alone˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You are only a warner for those who fear it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Your duty is only to warn whoever is in awe of it.

ayahs.textArabic: كَأَنَّهُمۡ يَوۡمَ يَرَوۡنَهَا لَمۡ يَلۡبَثُوٓاْ إِلَّا عَشِيَّةً أَوۡ ضُحَىٰهَا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaannahum yawma yarawnaha lam yalbathoo illa AAashiyyatan aw duhaha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It will be, on the Day they see it, as though they had not remained [in the world] except for an afternoon or a morning thereof.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On the Day they see it, it will be as if they had stayed ˹in the world˺ no more than one evening or its morning.
